summary: In a normal distribution, data clusters symmetrically around the mean, and the 68-95-99.7 rule tells you what percent falls within each standard deviation.
explanation: A normal distribution is symmetric and bell-shaped, centered at the mean, where mean = median = mode. The 68-95-99.7 rule (empirical rule) says approximately 68% of data falls within 1 standard deviation of the mean, 95% within 2 standard deviations, and 99.7% within 3. Because the curve is symmetric, exactly 50% of the data lies above the mean and 50% below. To find the percent above or below a specific cutoff that's a whole number of standard deviations from the mean, use the rule together with the symmetry: e.g., the percent below "mean + 1 SD" is \(50\% + 34\%=84\%\) (half the distribution, plus half of the middle 68%).
